probable_causes.test: Check layer height — should be max 75% of nozzle diameter

probable_causes.cause: Printing too fast — no time for proper layer bonding
probable_causes.test: Check print speed — reduce to 40-50mm/s and test

probable_causes.cause: Wet or degraded filament with poor material properties
probable_causes.test: Listen for popping/crackling and check filament brittleness

steps.instruction: Increase nozzle temperature by 5-10C increments until layers bond
steps.verification: Layers cannot be peeled apart by hand

steps.instruction: Reduce layer height to maximum 75% of nozzle diameter (0.3mm for 0.4mm nozzle)
steps.verification: Layer height setting updated in slicer

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed to 40mm/s for perimeters, 60mm/s for infill
steps.verification: Speed settings updated in slicer

steps.instruction: Dry filament at manufacturer's recommended temperature for 4-8 hours if moisture suspected
steps.verification: No popping sounds during extrusion after drying

problems.name: Too Much Cooling — Weak Layer Adhesion
problems.description: Excessive cooling prevents proper layer bonding, especially for high-temperature materials, resulting in weak prints that crack along layer lines
visual_symptoms: Layers separate easily when print is flexed or stressed
Print feels brittle despite using normally-strong material
Visible layer lines that are under-bonded on thick prints

probable_causes.cause: Full cooling fan speed used for materials that need reduced cooling (PETG, ABS, ASA)
probable_causes.test: Check slicer fan speed profile — PETG max 30%, ABS 0%

probable_causes.cause: Environment too cold — ambient air chilling print too fast
probable_causes.test: Check ambient temperature — should be 20C+ for PLA, 35C+ for ABS

probable_causes.cause: Fan duct directing cold air at already-printed layers
probable_causes.test: Inspect fan duct geometry and airflow direction

steps.instruction: Set fan speed by material: PLA 80-100%, PETG 20-30%, ABS/ASA 0%, Nylon 0%
steps.verification: Slicer fan speed profile matches material recommendations

steps.instruction: If printing in cold room, add enclosure or move printer to warmer location
steps.verification: Ambient temperature at least 18C

steps.instruction: Increase nozzle temperature by 5C to compensate for aggressive cooling
steps.verification: Layer adhesion test shows improved bonding

problems.name: Wrong Nozzle Size in Slicer
problems.description: Slicer is configured with a different nozzle diameter than the physical nozzle installed, causing severe under or over-extrusion
visual_symptoms: Lines much wider or narrower than expected from the configured nozzle size
Significant under-extrusion with large gaps, or over-extrusion with squished lines
Detail resolution does not match expectations for the configured nozzle

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle replaced but slicer not updated to match
probable_causes.test: Compare physical nozzle marking/size with slicer nozzle diameter setting

probable_causes.cause: Slicer profile loaded from a different printer with different nozzle
probable_causes.test: Check if current slicer profile was imported or copied from another machine

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle markings worn off making size unclear
probable_causes.test: Use a caliper on the nozzle hex or look up part number

steps.instruction: Check physical nozzle size — look for engraved size on hex flat, or measure orifice with calipers
steps.verification: Nozzle size confirmed (e.g., 0.4mm, 0.6mm)

steps.instruction: Update slicer printer profile to match physical nozzle diameter
steps.verification: Slicer nozzle diameter matches physical nozzle

steps.instruction: Ensure line width settings are appropriate (100-120% of nozzle diameter)
steps.verification: Line widths updated proportionally

steps.instruction: Re-slice model and reprint
steps.verification: Extrusion looks correct — lines the right width and well-bonded

problems.name: Infill Pattern Showing Through Walls
problems.description: The infill pattern is visible through the outer walls of the print, creating a textured or shadowed pattern on what should be a smooth surface
visual_symptoms: Infill grid or honeycomb pattern visible through perimeter walls
Dimples or indentations on outer surface matching infill pattern
Surface appears lumpy with regular spacing matching infill spacing

probable_causes.cause: Not enough perimeter walls/shells to hide infill
probable_causes.test: Check wall count in slicer — usually need 3+ walls to prevent show-through

probable_causes.cause: Infill percentage too high — infill pressing into walls
probable_causes.test: Check infill density — high density (80%+) can push into walls

probable_causes.cause: Printing infill before perimeters
probable_causes.test: Check slicer print order — perimeters should print before infill

probable_causes.cause: Wall line width too thin relative to nozzle
probable_causes.test: Check if wall width is set to less than nozzle diameter

steps.instruction: Increase wall count to 3-4 in slicer for smooth surfaces
steps.verification: Slicer preview shows 3-4 perimeter lines

steps.instruction: Reduce infill density to 20-40% for aesthetic prints
steps.verification: Infill density reduced in slicer

steps.instruction: Enable Print perimeters before infill in slicer if option available
steps.verification: Slicer ordering updated

steps.instruction: Ensure infill overlap with perimeters is 20-25% (not higher)
steps.verification: Infill overlap setting checked and corrected

problems.name: Uneven Bed Spring Tension
problems.description: Bed leveling springs have uneven tension causing the bed to tilt or bounce during printing, making consistent first layers impossible
visual_symptoms: Bed level changes significantly during a print (areas that were level become unlevel)
First layer inconsistent — too low in some areas, too high in others after leveling
Bed wobbles or vibrates when touched during print

probable_causes.cause: Springs compressed differently on each corner
probable_causes.test: Compress all springs to same height and check if bed stays level

probable_causes.cause: One or more springs weaker than others
probable_causes.test: Remove springs and compare compression force by feel or with scale

probable_causes.cause: Bed leveling thumbscrews at different heights
probable_causes.test: Count turns from flush on each thumbscrew — should be similar

steps.instruction: Home all axes (G28) and manually adjust all bed thumbscrews to equal spring compression
steps.verification: All corners at same approximate height

steps.instruction: Run bed leveling procedure and fine-tune each corner
steps.verification: Bed mesh shows uniform height across all corners

steps.instruction: Print a first layer test spanning the full bed and observe uniformity
steps.verification: First layer squish consistent across entire bed

steps.instruction: Replace springs with uniform high-tension springs (upgrade kit ~$5)
steps.verification: New springs installed with similar compression at all corners

steps.instruction: Alternative: replace springs with solid spacers and use Z offset for leveling (requires ABL)
steps.verification: Solid spacers installed, bed no longer moves between prints

problems.name: Over-Retraction Causing Gaps and Clogs
problems.description: Retraction distance or speed set too high causes filament to be pulled out of the melt zone, creating gaps and eventually a clog as molten filament contacts cooler regions
visual_symptoms: Gaps in perimeter lines immediately after travel moves
Frequent jams requiring cold pulls to clear
Thin or missing first few millimeters of line after retraction

probable_causes.cause: Retraction distance too high for direct drive (>2mm) or Bowden (>7mm)
probable_causes.test: Check retraction distance — direct drive max 1-2mm, Bowden max 4-7mm

probable_causes.cause: Retraction speed too fast causing grinding
probable_causes.test: Check retraction speed — 25-45mm/s is typical

probable_causes.cause: Too many retractions per mm — heat creep region damaged by repeated pulls
probable_causes.test: Check max retraction count or enable avoid crossing perimeters

steps.instruction: Reduce retraction distance: direct drive to 0.8-1.5mm, Bowden to 4-5mm
steps.verification: Retraction settings updated in slicer

steps.instruction: Reduce retraction speed to 25-35mm/s if experiencing grinding
steps.verification: Retraction speed setting updated

steps.instruction: Enable Avoid crossing perimeters / combing to reduce retraction frequency
steps.verification: Combing enabled in slicer

steps.instruction: Print retraction calibration tower (0mm to 3mm range) and find optimal value
steps.verification: Optimal retraction found with no stringing and no gaps

problems.name: Nozzle Dragging Through Print
problems.description: The nozzle drags through previously printed layers during travel or printing moves, knocking over the print or leaving gouges
visual_symptoms: Grooves or trenches visible in top surfaces where nozzle dragged
Print knocked over or shifted mid-print
Clicking sound of nozzle hitting solid material during moves

probable_causes.cause: Z offset too low — nozzle too close to bed on subsequent layers
probable_causes.test: Check if nozzle drags only on first layer or on all layers

probable_causes.cause: Over-extrusion causing layers taller than expected
probable_causes.test: Check flow rate / extrusion multiplier — should be 95-100%

probable_causes.cause: Z axis not advancing correctly (missed steps, loose coupling)
probable_causes.test: Measure actual Z height vs commanded — should match within 0.01mm

probable_causes.cause: Combing travel not avoiding perimeters — nozzle oozes and drags
probable_causes.test: Check if dragging only happens during travel moves

steps.instruction: Increase Z offset by 0.05mm if dragging occurs on first layer
steps.verification: Nozzle clears print surface during travel

steps.instruction: Reduce flow rate / extrusion multiplier to 95% and reprint
steps.verification: Layer height matches slicer layer setting on test print

steps.instruction: Enable Z-hop on retraction in slicer (0.2-0.4mm lift before travel)
steps.verification: Slicer shows Z-hop enabled for travel moves

steps.instruction: Check Z axis coupler on stepper shaft — tighten grub screws if loose
steps.verification: Coupler rigid, no slipping

steps.instruction: Check Z lead screw for debris or binding — clean and lubricate with PTFE lubricant
steps.verification: Lead screw turns smoothly by hand throughout range

steps.instruction: Calibrate Z steps per mm and verify layer heights are accurate
steps.verification: 10 commanded Z layers = measured 10x layer height

problems.name: Glass Bed — Poor Adhesion Without Coating
problems.description: Plain glass bed does not provide enough adhesion for most filaments without an adhesion aid, causing prints to slide or not stick at all
visual_symptoms: Print slides when nozzle moves over it
First layer not sticking to glass at all
Print pops off glass completely when bed cools

probable_causes.cause: No adhesion aid applied to glass
probable_causes.test: Check if glass is bare — no glue stick, hairspray, or coating

probable_causes.cause: Glass dirty or contaminated with release agent
probable_causes.test: Clean with IPA and test if adhesion improves

probable_causes.cause: Bed temperature not optimal for glass adhesion
probable_causes.test: PLA on glass works best at 55-60C and releases when cooled to 40C

probable_causes.cause: Z offset too high preventing filament contact with glass
probable_causes.test: First layer lines should be slightly flattened on glass

steps.instruction: Clean glass with IPA 90%+ thoroughly — do not touch surface after cleaning
steps.verification: Glass looks clean with no residue

steps.instruction: Apply thin layer of glue stick to glass in print area
steps.verification: Thin even film visible on glass surface

steps.instruction: Heat bed to 60C for PLA, 80C for PETG, 100C for ABS
steps.verification: Bed at target temperature

steps.instruction: Print and allow to cool to room temperature — PLA on glass releases cleanly when cold
steps.verification: Print releases without force when cold

problems.name: Hot End PTFE Liner Degraded
problems.description: PTFE liner inside the hot end has degraded due to high temperatures, causing clogs, off-gassing of toxic fumes, and poor print quality
visual_symptoms: Frequent unexplained clogs resistant to cold pulls
Unusual acrid smell during printing
Discolored filament on cold pulls even with fresh filament
Reduced flow that worsens over multiple prints

probable_causes.cause: Printing above 240C in a PTFE-lined hot end — PTFE degrades at 250C+
probable_causes.test: Check print temperature history — PTFE-lined hot ends should not exceed 240C

probable_causes.cause: PTFE liner has never been replaced after hundreds of hours
probable_causes.test: Check printer usage — PTFE should be replaced every 300-500 hours

probable_causes.cause: Hot end crash damaged PTFE tube at nozzle end
probable_causes.test: Remove and inspect PTFE tube for deformation, discoloration, or charring

steps.instruction: SAFETY: Stop printing immediately — degraded PTFE releases harmful fumes above 240C
steps.verification: Printing stopped

steps.instruction: Heat hot end to 230C and remove nozzle carefully with wrench
steps.verification: Nozzle removed, PTFE visible

steps.instruction: Remove old PTFE liner and inspect — discard if discolored, crushed, or charred
steps.verification: Old liner removed and inspected

steps.instruction: Install new Capricorn PTFE tubing (rated to 260C vs standard PTFE 240C)
steps.verification: New liner inserted fully and cut square at nozzle end

steps.instruction: Reinstall nozzle and hot tighten at 240C
steps.verification: Nozzle seated with no gap

steps.instruction: For regular high-temp printing, upgrade to all-metal hot end to eliminate PTFE entirely
steps.verification: All-metal hot end installed and tested

problems.name: Pressure Advance / Linear Advance Not Tuned
problems.description: Pressure advance (Klipper) or linear advance (Marlin) is not configured, causing corner blobs, rounding of corners, and inconsistent extrusion at speed changes
visual_symptoms: Blobs at corners where the nozzle slows down
Rounded corners instead of sharp 90-degree angles
Extrusion gaps after corners where nozzle accelerates
Inconsistent line width at speed changes

probable_causes.cause: Pressure advance not enabled or K-factor set to 0
probable_causes.test: Check Klipper pressure_advance value or Marlin M900 K value — should be non-zero

probable_causes.cause: K-factor too high causing under-extrusion at corners
probable_causes.test: Print PA/LA calibration pattern — look for gaps after corners

probable_causes.cause: K-factor too low causing over-extrusion (blobs) at corners
probable_causes.test: Print PA/LA calibration pattern — look for blobs at corners

steps.instruction: Print a pressure advance / linear advance calibration pattern
steps.verification: Calibration pattern printed

steps.instruction: Identify the line on the calibration print where corners are sharpest and line width most consistent
steps.verification: Optimal K value identified on the print

steps.instruction: For Klipper: add pressure_advance: [value] to printer.cfg and reload config
steps.verification: SAVE_CONFIG and Klipper shows new pressure_advance value

steps.instruction: For Marlin: send M900 K[value] via terminal and save with M500
steps.verification: Value saved and confirmed with M503

steps.instruction: Print a quality test and verify corners are sharp with no blobs
steps.verification: Corners sharp, no blobs, consistent line width

problems.name: Printing Too Slowly — Thermal Artifacts
problems.description: Printing too slowly causes thermal artifacts including melting of small features and drooping on overhangs that need to cool before the next layer is deposited
visual_symptoms: Small towers or pillars visibly melted or deformed at top
Overhangs on small features drooping despite adequate cooling fan
Thin walls bulging or warping outward

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too slow — next layer deposited on still-molten previous layer
probable_causes.test: Check print speed — anything below 15mm/s for small features risks thermal issues

probable_causes.cause: Minimum layer time too short for small cross-section features
probable_causes.test: Check minimum layer time setting in slicer cooling settings

probable_causes.cause: Cooling fan inadequate for feature size
probable_causes.test: Check fan speed and whether it ramps up for small layers

steps.instruction: Enable minimum layer time in slicer cooling settings (8-15 seconds recommended)
steps.verification: Slicer cooling settings show minimum layer time set

steps.instruction: Ensure cooling fan ramps to 100% for small cross-section features
steps.verification: Slicer shows fan speed linked to minimum layer time

steps.instruction: For tall thin towers: print multiple copies simultaneously so each part cools while other is printed
steps.verification: Two or more copies of the model on bed, spaced apart

steps.instruction: Add a sacrificial tower next to the part in the slicer to increase cooling time per layer
steps.verification: Sacrificial tower added alongside model in slicer
